explicit_text: I was born in Colombia, and it is where my life began and my family roots are. Throughout my early years, I moved between different cities in Colombia like Garzón, Huila, Bogotá, and Cali. Colombia is where my mother lived and where she eventually passed away from cancer. Even though I now live abroad, Colombia remains an important part of my identity and my family's history.

explicit_text: Garzón, Huila is the city in Colombia where I was born. It holds a special place in my heart as my hometown and where my mother returned to when she got sick. My early memories are tied to Garzón, and it was important for me to go back there to take care of my mother during her illness. It’s a place connected deeply to my family and my beginnings.

explicit_text: Cali is the city where my mother went when she got sick, and later where I moved to start working after she passed away. It was in Cali that I met my children's father, and we eventually got married. Cali marks an important chapter in my life as it was the place where my family began and where I raised my children before moving abroad.

explicit_text: The United States is where my youngest daughter moved first, and gradually all my children joined her here. We have been living here for more than five years now, trying to adapt and learn English. Life in the U.S. has been challenging but also full of opportunities, and my family is considering applying for citizenship. It’s a place of new beginnings for us, even though we sometimes feel the stress of language barriers and cultural differences.

explicit_text: Minnesota is where we have been living for over five years, and I really like it here. I appreciate the weather, the different seasons, and especially the kindness of the people. The way of life here is very good, with great transportation and respect for pedestrians, which is different from Colombia. My daughter asked if we wanted to stay here for our final years, and I said yes because I feel comfortable and happy in Minnesota.

Still, Dallas remains an important part of our family network in the United States.
